title: asplenium obovatum subsp. lanceolatum
intro: Asplenium obovatum subsp. lanceolatum, also known as the African Asplenium, is a species of fern native to South Africa. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Asplenium obovatum subsp. lanceolatum include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or paste to apply topically.
